channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: You should be holding at least 1 BTC. — 83 replies — 280.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
You should be holding at least 1 BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 83 • Updated: 2026-05-15 07:59 UTC

OP

Imagine not owning at least 1 BTC before institutions and governments vacuum the remaining supply while you’re still debating if Bitcoin is a scam… or just trading crypto without considering BTC long term.

Early replies (first 3)

62219945
>>62219942
Or I can trade alts (leveraged BTC) and actually make it instead of getting a 2x pump

62219995
Fuck. I have 2BTC . Is that enough bro?

62220027
>>62219995
no, i have 3. and it's not enough, we're just poor faggots

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62229475
>>62219942
I will own productive assets instead sar.

62229508
>>62229475
What's that? Is there any?

62229551
>>62229433
I don't have kids, it's over
>>62229508
Sp500 is beating our ass daily, tomorrow is making another ath kek

62229557
>>62229114
Don't trust
>>62229551
You should be having kids if you're over 30 now. Think of your fortune if you have any

62229561
>>62229551
What about forex?

62229570
>>62229557
Yeah, don't think I'm getting kids anytime soon and blew it with one of the few women I might had.
>>62229561
I'm an europoor and i don't do forex trading. I just hold and dca my wagie paycheck, but BTC has been shit this whole market run. And yes the opportunity cost once again is making no sense. Why not pile everything into boomer stocks, better risk and reward after all. I might need to diversify my bags and forget about Bitcoin for a while.
Godspeed

62229578
>>62229570
Be loyal ser and start a good family

62230004
I'm all in StrategyInc tho

62230021
>>62219942
What's supposed to be happening in the op pic

62231066
>>62225044
I got jewed of my own dip, gf was home all day yesterday and i didn't buy.
I hate women so much is unbelievable

62231072
>>62229557
Why though anon? It’s not like this is some random vaporware project. They already have partnerships with names like GoMining, Ledger, a16zcrypto and others around the ecosystem.

62231081
>>62230004
Why all in? : (

62231090
>>62229433
Good advice so far.

62232211
>>62222594
There's solution for this

62233500
>>62231072
is that legit?

62233508
>>62231066
You hate woman because you like man. Gayish bitcoiner

62233709
>>62233500
Yeah anon. There’s already almost 51k BTC staked in the protocol too, which is like $4B+ worth of BTC already participating around the ecosystem.

62233716
>>62223021
You can always keep slowly adding over time anon. Most people don’t even have close to 1 BTC.

62233736
>>62224655
it will be your opportunity as well.

62233806
>>62229570
I'm from Spain and I live well. Got small biz + doing crypto + forex + stocks. Life is good.

62233821
>>62233709
never though there's protocol exists like this

62233827
>buy btc
buy link
buy ICP
buy avax

62233835
>>62228833
And Ethereum is the godess

62233840
>>62228631
Hes just a homosexual and fears for the virginity of his anus

62233880
>>62233827

worst trash available
>UNGMI

62233986
>>62233821
Yeah anon, it already exists but I think a lot of people just still aren’t paying attention to the newer BTCFi infra yet. And it’s still pretty early too since the TBVs haven’t even launched yet, which should unlock even more native BTC use cases.

62233993
>>62225388
See this anon?

62233997
>>62224679
The funniest part is you probably think debasing fiat printed out of thin air is somehow more “real” than a globally traded asset with trillions in liquidity.

62234000
>>62230021
Gonna be dead broke without BTC lol.

62234021
>>62233806
Aren't you fucked with taxes and DAC8 now, I cannot buy anymore via a cex here

item.title: >never had a gf >never been on a date >now 36 >had jobs >go to gym >finally lose weight and have a BMI less than 25 >mak… — 60 replies — 240.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>never had a gf >never been on a date >now 36 >had jobs >go to gym >finally lose weight and have a BMI less than 25 >mak…

Open thread • Replies: 60 • Updated: 2026-05-15 08:11 UTC

OP

>never had a gf
>never been on a date
>now 36
>had jobs
>go to gym
>finally lose weight and have a BMI less than 25
>make multiple dating profiles on different apps
>zero matches

So I'm a confirmed subhuman. And, judging by the responses to ugly males who post their pics on Reddit and ask why they get no matches, normies openly laugh at me and think I deserve a low status life of slavery.

Why should I give a fuck about putting in effort at work or saving the west or whatever? Any sort of participation is a scam for someone like me.

Early replies (first 3)

62223666
>>62223664
>>>/adv/

62223769
>>62223664
depends how tall u are and if u have any real ugliness causing things like deformity or burn scars etc.

U probably are just average looking and if u arent 5'4'' u will find a gf

62224072
>>62223664
are you swiping on fat women or only the stacies?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62232980
>>62232830
Someone 5/10 is not going to get any luck on dating apps (which relies on looks). Will need to mix in social circle/familiarity irl for a chance. Below 5/10 got to accept you're better off with other avenues to find enjoyment like hobbies.

>>62232711
Where in person did you meet your wife?

62232984
>BMI 25
Anon you're fat.
>n-no I said less than
Anon you're fat. You need to be SKINNY. It's that simple. Also get the hair transplant.

62232990
Post your pics. You need a full spectrum of pictures. A mix of ones out with friends, you alone dressed up and ones dressed casual, a hobby of yours, etc. It sounds exhausting but if you’re living life right you should have a few. Women are going to imagine themselves in your lifestyle and if all your pictures are car selfies and pictures in a gym mirror shes going to think you’re boring. Women hate nothing more than they hate BORING

62232992
>>62231883
my obese balding friend actually got occasional tinder dates but they were ALWAYS with ugly obese women

you have to date in your league. whenever someone says they get literally zero matches i know they are going for 5/10's when they should be going for 1/10's and 2/10's.

62233004
>>62231869
no shit dumbass. if youve had meaningless sex with 50 women you've fried your brain romantically

62233012
kill fat people

62233014
bro online dating is HORRIBLE. Unless you're like the top 5% of hot guys out there it's gonna be a depressing humiliation ritual. You prob have to hire a professional photographer and make it look like you have a vibrant, exciting life with a bunch of staged pics. I used shitty selfies a decade ago, but now it doesnt cut it.

62233017
>>62233014
this. If not a chad you need to be fully optimized with pics and lifestyle. Otherwise irl, which probably means 1 girl opportunity every few months otherwise you spoil the well

62233024
>>62232652
This
It’s sickening once you look into it.
Women at large are getting blamed when half the women on there are fake profiles anyway. There’s plenty of fake men’s profiles now too, dedicated to romance scams. Which is a whole other issue, men and women being victims

62233040
>>62233017
>that ugly guy with super polished tryhard profile

this unironically gives girls the ick

62233064
>>62233040
they get the ick from the guy being ugly. Selecting photos where he looks most attractive is only going to help compared to bathroom selfies. If he does that he'll get 0 matches guaranteed.

62233065
>>62233040
If hes ugly they already have the ick

62233066
>>62233065
exactly. The most important tip is don't be ugly. 2nd is don't be autistic as fuck. 3rd actually talk to girls whether online or irl whatever.

62233115
>>62223664
Dating apps arent for dating they're for sluts to get their holes blown out by a small selection of chads and fuckbois.

62233200
>>62233115
>chads
>fuckbois
what's the difference?

62233241
>>62233200
Chads don't have to try because their appearance does everything. Fuckbois have to try hard to get women to fuck them. They still have fuck but the level of effort is completely different.

62233271
>>62223664
I posted my face on soc once when I was drunk.

Everyone gave me an 7 or 8/10. I still feel ugly though.

62233283
>>62231723
5/10 roasties only swipe on 8+

62233302
>>62233283
/thread

All women swipe 3+ points up. If you're only attracting morbidly obese women, down syndrome women and old fat single mothers, you're an above average looking guy.

These studies have already been done 1,000 times. Average looking guys get 1 match a month abs it's usually a fake/bot account or a 300+ pound clown with 20 fishhooks in its face.

62233310
>>62223664
your ugly ancestors got pussy somehow and so can you

62233355
>>62223664
Remember, for every beautiful person, there must be 5 ugly ones.

I don't make the rules.

62233408
>>62223664
Why do you want a girlfriend? If you want to LOVE a woman you are gay, sorry to tell you. You need to start niggermaxxing, learn to pump and dump

62233456
>>62223664
Go to Philippines, Moldova, or Gambia, depending on what race you prefer. You'll get more girlfriends than you know what to do with.

I'd post some pix of hot bare-titties Himba girls but the jannies get all assblasted about that for some reason.

62233560
>>62232980
>Where in person did you meet your wife?
GF of a friend introduced me to her.
This strategy requires having friends, I know that's too much to ask of GenZ nowadays.

62233715
>>62233271
i never posted on soc but i did on the site photofeeler where people rate your pic. Out of 120 rates i averaged to a 3/10

which makes sense since im khv at 30. I am way too scared to post my face on this site though thats crazy

62233733
you can't be this naive at 36, its surprising how many manchildren there are in the west still chasing dating and love and marriage when your zoomer generation have figured it out for the scam that it is

62233792
>>62233733
yep, one of the most popular streamers smashes his face with a hammer... lmfao

all of this was figured out over a decade ago but no one wanted to listen.

62234041
>>62232735
>social pariah wonders why he's not surrounded by a harem of qt3.14s
many such cases

62234043
>>62233715
I'm 32 and a virgin. Just dick in pussy/ass virgin. I've done everything else. Also 6'3" and slender/fit.

62234052
Maybe you shouldn't spend so much time around permavirgins (reddit)

item.title: CLARITY ACT PASSED — 25 replies — 100.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
CLARITY ACT PASSED

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-05-15 08:09 UTC

OP

CLARITY ACT PASSED

Early replies (first 3)

62231538
NO WAY

62231541
>>62231457
It hasn't even gone to senate yet retard

62231557
>>62231457
It passed a committee's approval, retard.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62231575
>>62231557
Next stop is the actual floor my nigger so this is huge

62231591
>>62231575
Just like your next step is being on the floor with something huge

62231681
>>62231591
Your mom is quite large

62231687
>>62231457

To the moon

62231715
>>62231681
i've been lurking in this thread since he made that post to make sure you didn't miss the easiest Your Mom layup of all time

thank you

62231721
bearish

62231732
techno feudalism ftw

62231747
>>62231457
who cares
t. Bitcoin

62232387
>>62231457
Cool narrative, cycles in control

62232401
>>62231541
>>62231557
it already passed the house. it passed the senate committee with bipartisan support. so you can take a wild guess what will happen when it goes to the senate and then back to the house a second time.

62232407
>>62232401
We are gonna be so fucking rich my nigger

62232414
>>62232401
So it's destined to fail?
Guess I'm selling everything then :(

62232429
>>62231687
Based retard

62232493
>>62231457
What does this actually mean for crypto? Are we gonna flip or flop

62232627
>>62231457
They're doing this so their super AI can be better predictive of the market

62232634
>>62232493
Jews are selling the news but I think it’s a fake out.

62232646
>>62232493
Means the keys for defi are handed over to big banks. Congratulations on this, really.

62233741
>>62232646
Just be thankful you have a chance to escape the underclass, wagie.

62233758
>>62232646
Good. Fuck everyone. Fuck all white people who love diversity. I’ll be laid up in a gated community with my harem while soijack covid vaccine takers can dodge bullets in the hood. Fucking pussy ass bitches ruined this country.

62233810
bullish
>>62232646
as planned

62233971
>>62232646

62234046
>>62232646
Defi is scammy trash and it always was. XRP was always the play, most of everything else was a scam, we tried to warn you, over and over and over and over and over and over. Jews always win. Get a fuckin clue guy.

item.title: Can you even make It as a non-jew, non-Ivy league ? — 33 replies — 53.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Can you even make It as a non-jew, non-Ivy league ?

Open thread • Replies: 33 • Updated: 2026-05-15 07:40 UTC

OP

I spent the past 4 months building and working on my startup. Scaled it up fairly quickly to nearly 20k USD monthly recurring revenue.

Of course as a typical AI startup I wanted to raise some capital to grow faster, hire and in general scale the company and make connections.

I reached out to a variety of investors, VCs and accelerators across USA and Europe but despite (in my opinion) really impressive and fast growth I struggle to even get responses from investors. My startup is solving a real issue and has had really good feedback from my business clients. Thing is I am not even asking for a crazy amount of money (I want to raise ~500K USD).

Honestly, can you even make it in Silicon Valley (and VC world in general) as a non-jew, non-Ivy league, Eastern European ?

Worst of all, I see all these garbage AI startups with no revenue getting 500K checks from YCombinator left and right and it just makes no sense. What am I doing wrong ? What am I not understanding ?

Early replies (first 3)

62228030
Have you tried y combinator?
Do you have connections with your state small business people?
Do your clients have glowing reviews written down and available to share?

62228036
Wrong question
What you should be asking is:
>”Can you make it if you have to deal in the real world?”

62228040
>>62228023
In short: yes, or at least I believe so.
Here's the thing anon; If you don't have the grit to make it in spite of all of the odds against us these days, you're NVGMI.
Maybe I'm wrong and we're both fucked. But I'm right about one thing for certain: even if we are fucked only someone who pushed any way would ever get there.
Now tell me what you're company does so I can steal your idea plz :3

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62228053
>>62228030
>Have you tried y combinator?
Yes, I applied 2 weeks ago for their Summer batch. Should maybe hear back June 5th. We'll see.
>Do you have connections with your state small business people?
Problem is I am in Europe and everyone has paper hands over here. Raising money is extremely difficult, most investors offer really bad conditions and EU/government funding is insanely time consuming, complicated and offers very little money.
>Do your clients have glowing reviews written down and available to share?
I am currently working on improving my businesses online presence. Since I am in B2B online presence was not really top priority for now. But I do have good feedback so far.

62228058
>>62228023
You have shitty social capital. If you were doing it right you just chat up the boys and Uncle Joe for your friends and family round. Shit goes well with the $500k they give you ($100k is basically just a nice watch or two weeks vacation so it's nbd with multiple people cutting checks) so Uncle Joe goes to his VC firm and introduces you and they take it from there.

Try networking. Actually meeting people and getting to know them.

62228079
>>62228058
>You have shitty social capital.
Yes, I do unfortunately. Truthfully, I have very few connections and all clients I have right now required insane amounts of effort to acquire, but I can see that this thing is scaling.
> If you were doing it right you just chat up the boys and Uncle Joe for your friends and family round.
Yes, I could probably gather quarter mil from friends/relatives. My goal here primarily is to establish connections in the VC world, which my family/friends have no connections to.
>Try networking. Actually meeting people and getting to know them.
That is what I focus on now, I just struggle to figure out the correct approach.

62228101
>>62228053
> I'm in Europe
Ah, sorry bro. I don't have much actionable advice then.
I will say though, 4 months is EXTREMELY young for investment in my experience. I work with small businesses and a handful of start ups. All of them were at least 3yrs old before they got their first round, some even 5+.
Get your social capital up, get marketing going, don't stop applying and reaching out.
> Correct approach
Go to events, introduce yourself and like 5 words about what you do, ask what other people do and really listen to them.
If you're smart you can figure out if they're a decent potential client. If you're not smart IDK what to tell you.

62228127
>>62228101
>All of them were at least 3yrs old before they got their first round,
Interesting. In what stages were these companies as far as revenue goes ? Any specific metrics you looked for ?

>4 months is EXTREMELY young for investment in my experience.
Yes, it is young. The reason I started reaching out to VCs this early is because that is the message many VCs/Accelerators put out there (make an MVP, get revenue, apply for funding early, scale, etc.).

>Go to events, introduce yourself
Thanks anon. I sort of came to the same conclusion earlier. Will be traveling quite a bit for variety of related events this year so hopefully something will come out of it.

62228128
>>62228023
Yeah idk talk to a bank if you have such good numbers. When I went on the VC circuit in the mid 20teens for a biotech startup the reality was anything involving “physical” technology was a huge Nono. All they wanted to invest in were wizkids who could crank out a billion dollar app on a laptop and some pizza.
AI is demanding in terms of chips, and “hiring people” is a major turnoff for investors.
I’m not saying it’s right, in fact it’s exactly why China is kicking our ass and taking names in literally every sector bar theoretical physics, but capitalist gonna maximalize capital.

Fuckers

62228139
>>62228023
not sure, but it might be worth getting a credit report from Dun & Bradstreet and then looking at how to improve your business credit score.

I don't know about VC but I'm pretty sure banks and other companies look at your D&B report. If you're not reporting earnings they sorta don't exist. The flip side is if you're reporting earnings you owe taxes. But at $20k per month I'd hope you're hiring accountants and paying taxes already.

a US biz needs to be reporting US earnings and paying US taxes to get on the radar.

62228143
Buy $TOSHI you dumb fuck
Memecoins are your only chance and desu Toshi is superior to all other memes. Nothing else has the upside potential and the groundwork laid.

62228171
>>62228023
also worth pointing out that banks and other investors often want 3 YEARS of earnings before investing or loaning money.

At 4 months you might be a really great investment, but you might also be a flash in the pan that will be gone in a year. Either way you're extremely risky to bet on because you're so new.

62228183
>>62228127
To clarify, I'm not in the investing industry. I'm a consultant for small/med businesses and that's as much detail as I would like to provide.
I'm not aware of their revenue at the time of their initial investments, but most were just recently breaking 1 mil annual revenue when I started working with them.
Starting to reach out this early is the correct thing to do, just don't get discouraged. If you're reaching out to the same investor a year from now, that would be encouraging to them that you're still going. Even if they never replied, it's still building rapport.
Hell, some people I know won't respond for months just because they're busy not because they aren't interested.

62230461
>>62228023
>Honestly, can you even make it in Silicon Valley (and VC world in general) as a non-jew, non-Ivy league, Eastern European ?
Obviously not. When did you think someone who doesn't go to the synagogue could make it?
You deal in the real world, goyim.

62230540
Hows you social media marketing/shilling? If you dont have an active and somewhat curated profile on xitter (esp. for tech related solutions), it will be very difficult to get signiticant exposure and get noticed by some VC bigwigs , as silly as it sound but a big part of startup financing is done via DM sliding today

Also try to expanding your MRR, if you still in Evropa then 20k MRR is an insanely good number and you can live like a poverty king here if you scale it decently, additionally with the European Digital Sovereignty shift you chances could be even better if you manage to market it smartly

62230548
>>62228023

What AI startup, retard?

What can you even show that Stanford/MIT grads and alumni can't do in a week?

Do you fucking have any idea what kind of talent pool is just in these two universities? And there are also Caltech, CMU, Cornell, etc.

And there tens of thousands of are ex-es: ex-googlers, ex-meta, ex-whatever.

So what have you to show? An ipython netebook with some pytorch shit?

Touch some grass, retard.

62230633
>>62228023
> 20k monthly recurring revenue
> can't get investors
What are you even talking about? What do you need investors for unless you your expenses are over your revenue? If you are just burning cash, that's the answer.

Nam to ne pizdi.

> Problem is I am in Europe and everyone has paper hands over here. Raising money is extremely difficult, most investors offer really bad conditions and EU/government funding is insanely time consuming, complicated and offers very little money.
So now you are getting to the core if the issue - yea, it's harder to get investment while you are in Europe.

>Yes, I could probably gather quarter mil from friends/relatives. My goal here primarily is to establish connections in the VC world, which my family/friends have no connections to.
That's what a friends & family round is anyway, makes it more likely to get VC money.

Again, if you actually have 20k revenue and you didn't just buy all your users and it's not all transient, you will get investments at the first major investment event you go to.

>>62230548
It doesn't matter what the tech is if he has 20k real revenue a month. The issue is that it's probably all bought clicks. Otherwise he would self fund.

62230796
>>62228036
And the answer is no. By definition practically, no.

62230980
>>62228023
>What am I doing wrong
You were born as a Straight, White Male.
>You have shitty social capital
This is to say, in the new Western Liberal system, only certain social pedigrees are allowed to advance.

62231243
>>62228023
Investors are herd animals. They wait for a lead investor to believe in you. Most don't understand the technical side of what you do and whether you or your business model are sustainable. You have no history (how you deal with difficult business problems), didn't move to a startup hub ("low effort"), you're not introduced by someone they know and trust (warm intro).
4 months provide not enough data points. $500k may be even too little, that's rather angel investment territory.
Rather build and grow your business than try to get VC money with the odds against you for now. Once you have some track record and significant $$$, more will listen.

Also (don't be discouraged by the author) this Friday evening read,

https://playbook.samaltman.com/

62231340
>>62230548
>What can you even show that Stanford/MIT grads and alumni can't do in a week?
OP here, Most of them have no revenue, just a shitty idea and shitty MVP. If you think scaling any AI startup is easy you are delusional and going to MIT or being ex-google is little advantage when getting clients, it only helps getting investors money. Having the revenue I have in that little time puts me in top 5% of all the startups. You are basically confirming what I was saying in the original post (am not a jew and/or didn't go to ivy-league).

>So what have you to show? An ipython netebook with some pytorch shit?
Real product, customers, demand and fast traction. Something that unironically most of the MIT/Ivy League fags don't have (most of them just have a shitty website and 0 revenue when they apply for funding.)

>>62230633
>What do you need investors for unless you your expenses are over your revenue?
To scale faster. Getting in these investor networks opens opportunities to deploy my product in more companies they already funded &have connections to. That is why most startups go for funding. Its actually not even about the money but about the doors it opens for you.

>Again, if you actually have 20k revenue and you didn't just buy all your users and it's not all transient, you will get investments at the first major investment event you go to.
I do and already mentioned to another anon I am going to bunch of events in SF later this year. The problem was cold outreach to investors has shown to mostly be a waste of time.

>It doesn't matter what the tech is if he has 20k real revenue a month. The issue is that it's probably all bought clicks. Otherwise he would self fund.
It's B2B sector I can't buy some "clicks" or users. You are making incorrect assumptions. I have real clients and contracts in place.

>>62231243
Thanks for the insight anon. This is actually useful advice. Thanks for the link as well, I read through it quite some time ago I believe.

62231399
>>62231340
>To scale faster. Getting in these investor networks opens opportunities to deploy my product in more companies they already funded &have connections to. That is why most startups go for funding. Its actually not even about the money but about the doors it opens for you.
This isn't an answer. I am very familiar with how ycombinator and other accelerators work. The reality is that you can "scale faster" if you can acquire customers that fast or want to monopolize the market. OR if you need large capital investments like a factory / data center.

Based on everything you said (which is very little), your only cost is hosting, services (OAuth, payments, geoloc, KYC, etc). None of those need funds. If you have 20k revenue in 4 months, you should easily be be at 200k by the end of the year without funding.

> It's B2B sector I can't buy some "clicks" or users. You are making incorrect assumptions. I have real clients and contracts in place.

If this is all true, ya tebe naidu investorov blyat zavtra. Ser'ezno, tvoya istoriya ne ochen svyazivaetsya.

Doxuya ludei xotyat investirovat v normalnie startapy, a to chto ty opisal, eto prymo axuetelno - tochno est kakaya ta zagvozdka o kotoroi ti ne govorish.

If you aren't a real slav, run that through GPT. But yea, put together a 10-slide powerpoint, go to an investor event and you will get funded for a mil or two in 5 minutes. You aren't telling us something, and your business isn't that secret. And if it's "that secret", then it's what the trolling anon said - you will get copied immediately.

I literally know a construction slav who is putting a mil into a "lost and found" app right now. I am sure he would rather invest in you if you aren't bsing.

62231430
>>62230540
>Hows you social media marketing/shilling?
Really poor unfortunately. Since I am in B2B and due to the nature of my product I had very little reason to market it online so far. X does make sense for AI/tech related stuff and I am already focusing on this.

>Also try to expanding your MRR
20k MRR but the profit is about 8-10k. I am putting most of it back in the business now.

>if you still in Evropa
My problem is just the shitty attitude, laziness and slow moving pace of the European market and people in general. The American market just moves and scales quickly. I had some really poor experiences with European-based investors as well unfortunately. Truthfully, I want to get out of here as soon as I have the chance. There is a reason why our founders move the fuck out of here.

62231482
>>62231399
>I am very familiar with how ycombinator and other accelerators work.
Honestly no, you don't sound really familiar. I spoke to a handful of people who get into YC and for many startups their first and/or major clients come from the YC network. The point is you can easily approach fellow YC startups and pitch your services. Not to mention the entire network is deeply connected into the entire Silicon Valley network and god knows how many other investors. It could be said the entire point of YC is to polish your startup in the 3 months and put you in front of investors on the "Demo Day" to raise more money &gain exposure to other businesses. It basically validates your entire startup and makes pitching a lot easier.

>You aren't telling us something, and your business isn't that secret.
since this is 4chan I intentionally don't want to disclose too much about the business because you could easily figure it out but there are many costs (token cost, some outsourcing, etc). With all the expenses I am looking at ~10k/month.

>go to an investor event
Yes, I mentioned to other anons that is what I will do. I relied on cold outreach for now and it showed very little results. Thanks anyway anon.

62231531
>>62231482
You are stating that you have a profit of 10k a month. What you haven't answered is what you need the money for. 10k a month is enough to do almost anything you want - you can hire a marketing team in Europe to run serious campaigns for less.

Like let's say you got 200-400k NOW at a 2 mil valuation, what are you going to do with it?

What you are describing this whole thread is a business that doesn't need any funding. You can outright hire a team with the money you have and you don't have any capital expenses.

Token cost is variable on business and directly tied to your model.

I assume you don't speak kazap/ hohol, correct?

>Honestly no, you don't sound really familiar.
lol

62231587
>>62231531
> What you haven't answered is what you need the money for.
I admit I have not really explained it clearly earlier. The money is not the priority right now. As you said I can self fund just fine. I could raise maybe 300k from friends/family if I wanted to if needed. The point is to get into the accelerator/VC networks which in turn makes it easier to pitch to their business networks &larger enterprises. TLDR I don't have the social proof and it is limiting the potential of the business.

>Like let's say you got 200-400k NOW at a 2 mil valuation, what are you going to do with it?
Mainly hire sales people and hire a few tech people to help deploy the tech for clients.

>I assume you don't speak kazap/ hohol, correct?
No, I am west slav.

62231654
>>62231587
> Mainly hire sales people and hire a few tech people to help deploy the tech for clients.
You have the money to do that now, though you might need some east slavs. You can get an east slav senior that speaks english, has a decade of experience, and can do whatever the fuck basic-ass shit you are doing to connect their enterprise to your AI/MCP middle layer and/or set up RBAC for your agents and get them "on the network" (which is not really true, since it will be VPN / ExpressRoute, but whatever).

> The point is to get into the accelerator/VC networks which in turn makes it easier to pitch to their business networks &larger enterprises.
That is literally YCombinator or similar - most VCs won't get you into those doors.

But yea, if you are really at that scale, I DON'T know how that works. I just know that people with 500 mil net worth generally won't be able to get you into those doors magically, BUT if those corporations really need the product, it should sell itself.

If you are expect to be in on the Oracle -> CoreWeave -> NVIDIA circle jerk, you are lying to yourself.

Note, while my posts are aggressive, I am actually not intentionally trolling. Saying what I think is objectively true. My only advice is you absolutely have money for a team, and if you got 300k from family, that's enough to pay a slav team for a year or two.

62231668
>trying to work within the system
KEKAROO
No wonder white men are so fucking poor and childless.

62231700
>>62231654

>That is literally YCombinator or similar - most VCs won't get you into those doors.
True. That is why I mostly gave up on European investors and solely focus on Silicon Valley based VCs and accelerators at this point.

I do appreciate your insights. I just happen to see it differently from my point of view and what the limiting factor seems to be when going forward and trying to scale. Thanks anon.

62233778
>>62228023
You should be able to access $250k of 0% credit for the first 12-24 months on business credit cards.

62233841
>>62228023
Cayman Islands have lot's of deals. Cayman Enterprise City. DILA Capital. Connections, etc. Post contact info, I work in wealth managment for HNWI and family offices, we're always open to deal flow.

62233926
Incredible, a good, constructive, helpful /biz thread.
>>62231482
I think I know what you're doing. If I am correct, there's very little moat in reality and entry barrier is zero, but you know how to do it, got in early and your moat will be whatever business relationships you have and whatever experience you build up. You need customer contacts fast, and here's my tip - once you're in with a customer, focus on their business optimisation. Do be an analyst for them: what else can I do for you? Regardless of your current product. What are they doing stupidly? How can you get that done in a smarter way? Also no BPM or bullshit. You're faster and smarter than large enterprises so you can help them identify and improve the stupid. Analyse for free and offer paid solutions.

62233999
B2B relies heavily on word of mouth referrals.

I personally give discounts to clients that refer my company, and offer discounts to new clients they refer. Not sure about tech, but in B2B services I've never had to advertise. My clients talk to other businesses a lot.

item.title: WILL THIS FUCKING MARKET EVER FUCKING CRASH!!!?!?!??!?!?!??!?!? — 15 replies — 42.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
WILL THIS FUCKING MARKET EVER FUCKING CRASH!!!?!?!??!?!?!??!?!?

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-05-15 07:55 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62233535
Something tells me it'll never crash again (nominally)

62233584
>>62233522
The so called "crash" will recover in 2 months lol

62233643
>>62233522
it's a self-reinforcing bull run. it won't crash ever, it just goes up parabolcally forever due to recursively compounding leverage

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62233652
>>62233643

62233654
>>62233652

62233657
>>62233522

62233659
>>62233657

62233675
>>62233522

62233679

62233688

62233761

62233781

item.title: Bitcoin or altcoins? — 98 replies — 36.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin or altcoins?

Open thread • Replies: 98 • Updated: 2026-05-15 05:36 UTC

OP

If you had to pick, what would you buy right now: BTC or altcoins? I’m not seeing much ROI in alts lately, even utility or memecoins.

Early replies (first 3)

62195293
>>62195275
Bitcoin until 120k

62195300
you can't time the market I thought like >>62195293
last time, bitcoin to 70k and sell half and go into alts and then 2021 alts just fucking died for a year.

The alts that pumped are things that haven't gone parabolic yet, or big projects like Solana with glowie money attached to them. Ones like Link are not going to 1000 regardless of cope.

/biz/ will pretty much only give you bad info too. I've found maybe 3 coins here that were Guaranteed returns, everything else I had to research myself. In fact I remember last year posting proof that CRCL stock was going to 10x on IPO and got XRP schizos fudding me out of taking a life changing position.

I actually hate the fuck out of baggies.

62195373
>>62195293
Why 120k only?? Why not 1M?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62210047
>>62209304
but when?

62210050
>>62209291
hold and stake

62211194
>>62207855
I've got some hbar in my portfolio

62211429
>>62211194
hbar going down to 2c before it ever gets a chance at real traction

62211440
>>62195275
>Chainlink is the future

62211870
>>62211440
Hahaha

62211903
>>62195275
Everyone moved to BTC of course, I dumped my ETH for BTC too, I used to hold BTC because of staking but now I can stake BTC natively thanks to TBV and the likes

62211915
>>62195275
I’d rather keep stacking BTC, then put it to work via native staking / yield layers (like Babylon-style setups) instead of chasing dead charts and hoping for a narrative pump.

62211928
>>62209385
Ok as you say so

62211932
>>62211429
Hbar is okay to hold and buy

62212034
>>62195275
More potential upside on alts for the next six months. Specifically memes right now.

62213245
>>62211440
What's your price target anon?

62214696
>>62211932
All in or?

62216973
>>62211915
It's worth considering anon

62216987
>>62195275

Polkadot

62220595
>>62209854
Are you sure?

62224657
>>62216987
Why polkadot?

62224666
cny/usd is > .147 now w/trump headed to china. every penny that can chase risk on is gonna get horned up immediately. The ONLY thing the US has left is the dollar as a w3@p0n. Plus, i can't afford any more makers mark bottles unless my alts start pumping w/haste, so...

62224894
>>62224666
Crazy

62224972
>>62224666
We're cooked

62226444
>>62224666
Risk-on driven by geopolitics, liquidity, and alcoholism. Peak crypto posting.

62228393
>>62216987
>ICP

62228841
chainlink

62229090
>>62216987
he's also pedo

62232214
>>62228393
And why

62232231
>>62195275
>buying the top of the bull trap
Never change, /biz/

62232233
>>62195275
Alts
If I'm already going for a risky meme-asset like crypto I might as well go for broke. Bitcoin already had its big run, now it's just stock market levels of gains with far greater risk.

62232268
>>62232231
Also, this threads riddled with bots holy shit

62232289
>>62195275
Altcoins for profit, Bitcoin for stable investments.

62233740
>>62228841
I'm not holding this

item.title: Taxes Thread — 30 replies — 20.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Taxes Thread

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-05-15 06:50 UTC

OP

Can any USA tax lads please go more in depth and in detail about how to set up a tax efficient business+trust?

Particularly how to be the most tax efficient with a business that is also protected by within a trust? S-Corp or C-Corp? Both in the same state? Offshore? How do you avoid double taxation?

Most importantly, how would you invest personal and business income in long term stocks that you select?

How would you deal with income you got from an actual job somewhere which is not part of the your business but that you wanted protected by a trust and invested in your choice of stocks?

What would all the target tax rates be?

Please detail everything. Provide tips and cover all risks, etc. I have tried studying this on my own but the scenario I want in which all income from business and personal in custom selected stocks while protected by trust and taxed as small as possible, complicates things.

Early replies (first 3)

62218156
>>62218140
consult a great tax attorney and a cpa

62218211
>>62218140
if you make a personal s-corp you can expense everything in CA like paying for your own health insurance for example but you pay a minimum franchise tax of $800 even if you made nothing so not great for side-hustles owned by me that made nothing

62218357
>>62218140
Formula F1. If you can spare 10m, sponsor an F1 team and then expense your entire life

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62218371
>>62218140
Totally off-topic but it really makes me seethe that I’m really out here paying 25 percent of my income to pedophiles, then on top of that I get taxed on investments, purchasing anything, property, etc. Our ancestors tarred and feathered tax collectors over a 2 percent tax on stamps and now we work for three months out of the year for the government.

62218405
>>62218211
This is basically the grift. You set up an S-Corp and deduct everything as a “business expense.”

It is also illegal to claim a business deduction for personal expenses, but this never stopped black people on TikTok from expensing the lease payments on their Chryslers.

62218495
>>62218140
I hate redditors and normies and low iq's. Pic related is such a retarded analogy. The company gets tax deductions because those expenditures were related to income generation. A person can do the same thing by getting tax deductions on things deemed necessary for work or income generation. You spending your income on overpriced doordash is not related to income generation.

62218507
>>62218357
Yeah just pay 10m to save 4m in taxes goy. Not a very smart jew. This is only worth it if you were going to spend money to live that lifestyle anyway, or you needed the marketing regardless.

62218524
the business spent that 3.9B on salaries to all the retards making 60k

62218651
I paid like 95k in income tax then got clocked for 4k more when I did my taxes.

62218760
>>62218140
Unless you make serious cash, this is the best you can hope for.

62218844
>>62218495
huh? i can't work hungry

62218962
>>62218140
Talk to @TheJerzWay on X (he will require payment), but he's a pro. He'll probably also recommend getting your REPS and residency in Puerto Rico.

62219425
>>62218140
Just don't pay your taxes
Most of them go towards jews and the rest goes towards trying to bring in as many third world subhumans to replace you as possible
It is probably the most cucked thing you can do in the current age, especially in the U.S
Anyone who disagrees with this should respond with "I am a good goy cuck" because there's no rationalisation or argument to make what I'm saying any less true.

62219490
>>62218140
>That's not–
You don't pay taxes at all, do you?
You don't know what a Standard Deduction is, do you?
>File by May 15th
Yeah, whoever wrote this has never paid taxes in their life.
>>62218507
>This is only worth it if you were going to spend money to live that lifestyle anyway,
He was. They all do. This is "Hollywood accounting". But now he can use his business to fund it with no risk to his personal assets.

62219495
>>62218844
Your basic needs are covered by the standard deduction.

62220996
>>62218357
Good job shlomo!

62221525
>>62218140
Tell me you don't know the fucking difference between a W2 and a 1099 without telling me.

62223970
>>62218405
Ben affleck explains how to do this in the Accountant movie.

62224000
>>62218405
Yes, but ultimately this just lets you live an inflated lifestyle at a discount. If you don't really want a G wagon, even making it tax deductible doesn't really help because you've still got to pay for the non deductible amount. And if you don't have a business that lets you pretend like maybe you need it (ie. A real estate agent taking wealthy clients to see homes)

62224006
>>62218140
>60k income
>58k expenses
yeah no wonder the person who made this meme doesen't understand taxes

62226707
>>62221525
I don't know the fucking difference between a W2 and a 1099.

62229176
>>62226707
landlords in image kek

62229320
>>62218357
AI slop.

62229358
>>62226707
>landlords
Too obvious, I am not sure you'll get anyone.
Funny, though.

62232808
>>62226707
Based landlords.

62232996
>>62229358
>I am not sure you'll get anyone
this image caused multiple meltys form tourists and twitterfags

62233708
Toby mathis has a lot of good tax tips for rich people on youtube. Its alot of good education but hes ultimately just shilling his firm. I dont know that I would use them, but its good for inspiration

62233719
>>62218140
the main idea is to dump all profits into growing the business so you don't pay income tax and at worst pay cap gains.

then you take your growing business and borrow against it, writing off the interest and fees of the loan as business expenses. Then you pay the loans to yourself in the form of company vehicles and housing and a very small salary which is just enough to pay back the loans and the tiny bit of income tax owed.

this is not tax advice, you should consult a tax professional. I'm not even sure what I'm saying is legal. I am sure it's what every rich person is doing though.

62233724
>>62233719
also there is a minimum amount you have to pay yourself depending on your field and the size of your business. Because the IRS is on to this trick. But the minimum salary for most people is pretty dang small.

again, not tax advice. consult a professional.

62233912
>>62233719
You should absolutely do this. This IS tax advice. I'm totally a tax expert. Just trust me bro.

XMR

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-05-15 06:54 UTC

62213668

62213696
>>62213668
You are cattle.

62213702
>>62213696
No, I am cashflow that is never going to have a demand for your bags. No matter what you baggies advertise

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62214009
>>62213668
I made it through XMR, it's an excellent opportunity for anyone who listens.

62216189
I like the coin

62216387
>>62214009
Same I dumped on newbaggies when we hit 800. Years of accoomoolating paid off.

62216481
>>62213665
Yeah but what can I buy with it? Where do I sell it for fiat?

62217118
>>62216481
Check monerica, you can buy pretty much anything.

62217182
>>62216481
>Yeah but what can I buy with it?
Anything. I'm currently buying work supplies for my business with XMR. That compounded with how much I've made my business is basically free and I'm making a couple thousand dollars a week.

62222692
>>62214009
based
sound money, safe mode

62222705
>>62217182
based
I've been shilling it in Lebanon, and trying to educate everyone
fuck central banks
fuck fiat
(((keynesians))) deserve the rope

https://www.france24.com/en/live-news/20210601-lebanon-crisis-among-world-s-worst-since-1850s-world-bank

never forget

62222769
>>62222705
Glad to see you're still around

62222781
>>62222769
thank you my friend
we have a saying here, roughly translates to:
if God is with us, who can be against us

62222813
>>62222781
Lovely, could've come straight from the apodeipnon

62223307
>>62222705
Fuck Israel too

62223522
How do I buy this dogshit and bury it in a ledger?
There was the wagyu hyperliquid thing but I don't trust that nigger

62223640
>>62223307
based

if you got some extra XMR to send I wouldn't mind
>>62214009
only asking cause I saw this
>>62222813
Christ is king

62224485
>>62223742
When we hit 500 I'll send 0.1 XMR, screenshot this

62226010
I like the coin

62226990
>>62224485
thank you friend

62227005
newfags still haven't learned what a monero spike means

62229871
>>62223742

62232296

62232605
>>62227005
Tell us

62233922
>>62232296

item.title: I read this article a while back, it said the Microsoft employs more millionaire secretaries than any other company in t… — 2 replies — 0.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
I read this article a while back, it said the Microsoft employs more millionaire secretaries than any other company in t…

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-05-15 05:41 UTC

OP

I read this article a while back, it said the Microsoft employs more millionaire secretaries than any other company in the world. they chose stock options over Christmas bonuses. It was a good move. I remember there was this photo of one of the groundskeepers standing next to his Ferrari. You see shit lke this, and you just can't see. Makes you think its possible even easy. And you turn on the TV and there's just more of it. The 87 million dollar lottery winner. That kid actor who just made 20 million on his last movie. The Internet stock that shot through the roof. You could have made millions off it if you just got in early. And that's exactly what I wanted to do. Get in. I didn't want to be an innovator. I just wanted to make the quick, easy buck. I just wanted in. The Notorious BIG put it best. "Your either slingin crack rock, or you got a wicked jump shot." Nobody wants to work for it anymore. There's no honor in taking that after-school job at Mickey-Dee's. The honor's in the dollar kid. So in the white boy way of slingin crack rock, I became a stockbroker.

Early replies (first 3)

62232431
N

62233749
I

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: /smg/ — 418 replies — 24.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
/smg/

Open thread • Replies: 418 • Updated: 2026-05-14 15:12 UTC

OP

Real world edition

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners:
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101:
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc smg:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

Old
>>62223487

Early replies (first 3)

62224553
we dont need another thread their is one at 100 now bugger of lad

62224574
>>62224553
This one's going to 420 ATH

62226335
Anon should be recycling these threads. It’s good for the environment

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62230538
>>62230527
we're just having a bit of fun in here ;)

62230539
>no Trump shitposts while he's on holiday
Sad! I want more wild swings from ramblings with no actual follow through

62230542
>>62230530
check this out

62230545
>>62230532
That's the issue. Inflation could bring rate cuts and then events like the yen carry trade unwinding

62230554
>>62230545
>That's the issue.
Future issue. A whiplash is a given at this point, might as well ride the wave till it comes

62230555
CAM ON SEVENTY FIVE HUNNED

62230556
>>62230527

Unironically might buy some since the low iq’s on stocktwits are openly bag holding

62230557
>>62230527
>are these all the same poster?
Clearly not. I wouldn't touch this hot garbage (American Fusion? Really?) even if it was available on Robinhood.
Penny Meme Stock Pump and Dumps are obvious, but adding country flags to posts would make it even more obvious, Saar!

62230558
Judging my the lack of mentions of my ticker....and someone else complaining about Sandisk....I will not check my port....

62230559
WE DID IT SMIGGIE WIGGIES

7,500 BIG ONES ON SPEE 500

CONGRATULATIONS TO EACH AND EVERY ONE OF YOU

62230561
LET THE BAKERS DEAL IN THE REAL WORLD

62230562
>dow over 50

62230565
>>62230527
>they think they aren't one of us
oh no no hivemind bros

62230568
HULK HOGAN
WE COMIN FO YOU NIGGA

62230572
this is how vindictive nips are
dumped a stock 20% today because the company is opening a plant in the us

62230573
I am still making money off of sneedisk
thank you for your attention to this matter

62230576
>>62230562

62230577
>>62230562
Yeah, but this time we don't have some useless cunt announcing it (I already fucked her)

62230579
SP500 HITS 7500

62230581
>>62230572
Why would massively increasing their wage costs be bullish?

62230585
>>62230584
WOULD

62230586
>>62230584
PamGODS won

62230587
>>62230576
>>62230584
build it twice as high

62230590
Bollinger band DEEZ NUTS

62230591
>>62230581
they had great earnings, and announced expansion. it's not that the earnings were priced in

62230592
>>62230573

62230594
>the /smg/ to end all /smg/‘s

62230596
>>62230595
>>62230595
>>62230595

62230597
This is the last /smg/ ever
